note: One-sentence World Cup history per nation, each under 20 words and grounded in that team's 'X at the FIFA World Cup' Wikipedia page (records fetched 2026-07-04). Written to complement - not repeat - the editorial intro (countryIntros.json). Source: Wikipedia World Cup records + synthesis. Powers the second typewriter line in the Atlas panel.

history.Argentina: Three-time champions, crowned again in 2022 when Messi finally lifted the trophy that had eluded him.
history.Spain: One crown, won in 2010 - outlasting the Netherlands in extra time for a first title.
history.France: Champions in 1998 and 2018, and finalists again in 2022 - a squad that never stops reloading.
history.England: One title, 1966, every match at home - still the only host to win it that way.
history.Portugal: Third in 1966 behind Eusébio's nine goals, still chasing a first final in the decades since.
history.Brazil: Present at every World Cup ever played, and a record five-time champion of the world.
history.Morocco: In 1986, the first African side to top a group and reach the knockout rounds.
history.Netherlands: Three finals - 1974, 1978, 2010 - and still the finest side never to be crowned.
history.Belgium: Played the very first World Cup match in 1930; peaked at third place in 2018.
history.Germany: Four-time champions with more podium finishes than any nation in the tournament's history.
history.Croatia: Runners-up in 2018 and twice third - extraordinary for so small a nation.
history.Colombia: Quarter-finalists in 2014, inspired by James Rodríguez's Golden Boot - their deepest run yet.
history.Mexico: Twice quarter-finalists on home soil, then seven straight Round-of-16 exits - always knocking on the door.
history.Senegal: Beat reigning champions France on their 2002 debut and stormed to the quarter-finals.
history.Uruguay: Two-time champions who silenced 200,000 Brazilians in the 1950 Maracanazo.
history.United States: Semi-finalists at the 1930 debut, and the giant-killers who stunned England in 1950.
history.Japan: Round-of-16 regulars who toppled both Germany and Spain at the 2022 World Cup.
history.Switzerland: Three-time quarter-finalists in the game's early decades, still the side nobody enjoys drawing.
history.Iran: Seven appearances without a knockout round, but 1998's win over the USA still resonates.
history.Turkey: Third place in 2002, a dazzling breakout on only their second appearance.
history.Ecuador: Reached the Round of 16 in 2006, the high point of five Andean campaigns.
history.Austria: Third in 1954, heirs to the fabled 1930s Wunderteam.
history.South Korea: Co-hosts who charged to fourth in 2002 - the first Asian side to reach a semi-final.
history.Australia: Twice into the Round of 16 since crossing from Oceania to Asia in 2006.
history.Algeria: Beat West Germany in 1982, then reached the Round of 16 in 2014.
history.Egypt: Africa's World Cup pioneers in 1934, breaking into the knockout rounds at last in 2026.
history.Canada: Goalless across 1986, Canada finally scored through Alphonso Davies in 2022, and now co-host for the very first time.
history.Norway: Beat Brazil en route to the 1998 Round of 16, now back after 28 years.
history.Ivory Coast: Drogba's golden generation fell short, but 2026 delivered a first-ever knockout appearance.
history.Panama: Felipe Baloy's strike in the 2018 debut gave the nation its first World Cup goal.
history.Sweden: Runners-up as hosts in 1958 and twice third - a Nordic side of genuine pedigree.
history.Czechia: As Czechoslovakia, twice runners-up in 1934 and 1962 - a proud Central European lineage.
history.Paraguay: Quarter-finalists in 2010, the peak of a hard-nosed, combative World Cup tradition.
history.Scotland: Eight appearances, never past the group - yet Archie Gemmill's 1978 solo goal lives forever.
history.Tunisia: The first African side to win a match, in 1978, and toppled France in 2022.
history.DR Congo: As Zaire in 1974, the first sub-Saharan African nation to reach a World Cup.
history.Uzbekistan: Sealed a maiden qualification in 2025, unbeaten through the second round.
history.Qatar: The first host nation ever to lose all three group matches, in 2022.
history.Iraq: One appearance, in 1986 - and both their qualifications have come when Mexico hosted.
history.South Africa: First host ever eliminated in the group stage, in 2010; reached the knockouts in 2026.
history.Saudi Arabia: Al-Owairan's 1994 solo wonder and the 2022 shock of champions Argentina define the Green Falcons.
history.Jordan: Debutants in 2026, arriving after a cruel 2014 playoff loss to Uruguay.
history.Bosnia and Herzegovina: Džeko marked their 2014 debut with a goal; 2026 brought a first knockout appearance.
history.Cape Verde: Held Spain and reached the 2026 knockout rounds on their maiden appearance.
history.Ghana: Seconds from becoming Africa's first semi-finalists in 2010, denied by Suárez's handball.
history.Curaçao: The first non-sovereign side from the Americas to qualify, topping its group in 2025.
history.Haiti: In 1974, Emmanuel Sanon's goal ended Dino Zoff's record shutout streak against Italy.
history.New Zealand: The only unbeaten team at 2010 - three draws - yet still out in the group.
